explanation: The command modifies a registry key in a suspicious path but uses a legitimate value (explorer.exe), which is a common legitimate use case. This could be a safe mutation to test detection boundaries.
This command uses a legitimate registry location (Run key) and a benign executable name. It tests whether the Sigma rule triggers on benign activity in a suspicious path.
This command modifies a registry key in a suspicious path but sets a legitimate value (TamperProtection=1), which is a known Windows Defender configuration. It tests detection specificity.
This command modifies a registry key in a suspicious path with a known legitimate Windows OOBE configuration. It tests whether the rule falsely triggers on valid system configuration.
This command modifies a suspicious registry path with a legitimate Shell value. It tests whether the rule can distinguish between benign and malicious activity in the same location.
